savage_findings.primary_violation: 100% of commands violate CLAUDE.md complexity limits
savage_findings.efficiency_disaster: Average 25.2 tokens per line of useful content
savage_findings.philosophical_critique: These commands embody the exact opposite of CLAUDE.md principles - they're abstractions for the sake of abstractions, wrappers around wrappers, with thinking about thinking about thinking.
savage_findings.recommendation: BURN IT ALL AND START OVER - seriously, a simple bash script would be more effective

comparative_benchmarks.efficiency_ratio: Simple solutions are 39x shorter, 65x less complex, 47x fewer tokens, 3x more maintainable

immediate_actions: Replace context-enhanced-executor with 'read docs; run command'
Burn intelligent-refactor-session and use IDE refactoring tools
architectural_principles: Follow CLAUDE.md complexity limit of 5 (currently averaging 65.2)
Eliminate meta-thinking loops - they add zero value
Remove XML markup soup - this isn't JSP from 2003
Write commands that DO things, not commands that think about doing things
If you need to think about your thinking, you're overthinking
